senses.definition: adjective | əˈlab(ə)rət | involving many carefully arranged parts or details; detailed and complicated in design and planning: elaborate security precautions | elaborate wrought-iron gates. • (of an action) lengthy and exaggerated: he made an elaborate pretense of yawning.
develop or present (a theory, policy, or system) in detail
examples: the key idea of the book is expressed in the title and elaborated in the text

senses.definition: add more detail concerning what has already been said
examples: he would not elaborate on his news

senses.definition: produce (a substance) from its elements or simpler constituents
examples: many amino acid and peptide hormones are elaborated by neural tissue

notes.content: late 16th century (in the sense ‘produced by effort of labor’, also in elaborate (sense 2 of the verb)): from Latin elaborat- ‘worked out’, from the verb elaborare, from e- (variant of ex-) ‘out’ + labor ‘work’.

phraseGroups.rawContent: elaborateness | əˈlab(ə)rətnəs | noun elaborative | əˈlabəˌrādiv, ēˈlabəˌrādiv, -rətiv | adjective elaborator | əˈlabəˌrādər, ēˈlabəˌrādər, -rātə | noun
phraseGroups.title: DERIVATIVES
